_comment: Hand-maintained content for the consumer index.md router. The guide manifest here is the source of truth for index.md and the catalog Topics; keep it in sync with the authored guides under CameraKit.docc/guides/.

guides.title: Overview
guides.summary: What CameraKit is; the engine as an actor; the lane model; the lifecycle model.

guides.title: Getting started
guides.summary: Install, permissions, and the construct → open → preview → capture → close order of operations.

guides.title: Lifecycle
guides.summary: Forwarding app lifecycle phases to the engine; what each phase reconciles to.

guides.title: Preview
guides.summary: The processed and tracker preview lanes and how to render them in any UI framework.

guides.title: Capturing stills and video
guides.summary: Two ways to capture a graded still, recording, output paths, and saving to Photos.

guides.title: Controlling the camera
guides.summary: Exposure, focus, white balance, zoom, resolution, and region-of-interest crop, bounded by capabilities.

guides.title: Image processing
guides.summary: GPU color adjustments applied to all delivered color output.

guides.title: Calibration
guides.summary: White- and black-balance calibration and reading the result.

guides.title: Observing state and errors
guides.summary: The event streams, session state, errors, and automatic recovery.

guides.title: Advanced: zero-copy consumers
guides.summary: Opt-in zero-copy frame consumption from Swift or native code.

capabilities.name: Lifecycle
capabilities.what: Keep the camera correct as the app moves between foreground, inactive, and background by forwarding one phase signal.

capabilities.name: Preview
capabilities.what: Display the live camera feed; choose the processed or tracker lane.

capabilities.name: Still capture
capabilities.what: Capture a graded photo — either a snapshot of the live stream or a fresh one-shot ISP capture.

capabilities.name: Video recording
capabilities.what: Record video to a file and optionally save it to the Photos library.

capabilities.name: Camera settings
capabilities.what: Set exposure, ISO, focus, white balance, and zoom within the ranges the device reports.

capabilities.name: Resolution and region-of-interest
capabilities.what: Select the capture resolution and apply a true sensor-region crop, expressed in active capture-resolution pixels.

capabilities.name: Image processing
capabilities.what: Adjust brightness, contrast, saturation, black level, and gamma applied to all delivered color output.

capabilities.name: White- and black-balance calibration
capabilities.what: Run gray-world white-balance and black-balance calibration and read the result.

capabilities.name: State, errors, and recovery
capabilities.what: Observe what the camera is doing, handle errors, and rely on automatic interruption recovery.

capabilities.name: Zero-copy frame consumers
capabilities.what: Consume raw frame sets with zero copy from Swift or native code (advanced).

conventions: The engine is an actor — every call is `async` and must be `await`ed.
Image processing applies to all delivered color output — the processed stream, the tracker lane, and both still captures.
Event streams do not replay — subscribe before or around `open()`.
`SessionCapabilities`, returned by `open()`, bounds every setting and crop.
